2604.12767.title: CLASP: Class-Adaptive Layer Fusion and Dual-Stage Pruning for Multimodal Large Language Models
2604.12767.paper_summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) suffer from substantial computational overhead due to the high redundancy in visual token sequences. Existing approaches typically address this issue using single-layer Vision Transformer (ViT) features and static pruning strategies. However, such fixed configurations are often brittle under diverse instructions. To overcome these limitations, we propose CLASP, a plug-and-play token reduction framework based on class-adaptive layer fusion and dual-stage pruning. Specifically, CLASP first constructs category-specific visual representations through multi-layer vision feature fusion. It then performs dual-stage pruning, allocating the token budget between attention-salient pivot tokens for relevance and redundancy-aware completion tokens for coverage. Through class-adaptive pruning, CLASP enables prompt-conditioned feature fusion and budget allocation, allowing aggressive yet robust visual token reduction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CLASP consistently outperforms existing methods across a wide range of benchmarks, pruning ratios, and MLLM architectures. Code will be available at https://github.com/Yunkaidang/CLASP.
2604.12767.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）因視覺標記序列中的高冗餘性而遭受相當大的計算開銷。現有的方法通常通過單層視覺Transformer（ViT）特徵和靜態修剪策略來解決這個問題。然而，這種固定配置在多樣化的指令下往往顯得脆弱。為了克服這些限制，我們提出了 CLASP，一種基於類別自適應層融合和雙階段修剪的即插即用標記減少框架。具體而言，CLASP 首先通過多層視覺特徵融合構建類別特定的視覺表示。然後，它執行雙階段修剪，在關注顯著的樞紐標記和關注冗餘的補全標記之間分配標記預算。通過類別自適應修剪，CLASP 實現了基於提示的特徵融合和預算分配，允許激進但穩健的視覺標記減少。大量實驗表明，CLASP 在各種基準、修剪比率和 MLLM 架構中始終優於現有方法。代碼將在 https://github.com/Yunkaidang/CLASP 上提供。
2604.12767.author: Yunkai Dang et.al.
2604.12767.authors: Yunkai Dang, Yizhu Jiang, Yifan Jiang, Qi Fan, Yinghuan Shi, Wenbin Li, Yang Gao
